data.title: South Korea's Kospi Losses Led by Defense, Shipbuilding Stocks
Chinese ethane imports to rebound as US lifts export restrictions
data.description:
Imports of ethane feedstock for Chinese petrochemical plants are set to rebound in August after the US Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) removed export restrictions on ethane shipments to China, said Chemweek.

“Loading will be resumed soon, partly compensating for the reduction in June due to the restrictions,” said a source at an ethane-fed steam cracker in eastern China.

“Lifting the restriction is simply a matter of timing, as disrupting trade flows hurts both China and the US. One side needs to buy, while the other needs to sell,” said a Shanghai-based chemical analyst.

Leading US ethane exporters Energy Transfer LP and Enterprise Products Partners LP filed statements on July 2 indicating they received letters from the BIS stating that the previously required licenses for the export, reexport or transfer (in-country) of ethane to parties in China, including Chinese military end users, have been rescinded, effective immediately.

Due to the restrictions, which BIS notified companies of in late May and early June, ethane cargoes loaded from the US to China were suspended from May 28 to June 17. Some cargoes loaded in June were diverted to other destinations, such as India, despite China being the usual destination, according to S&P Global Commodities at Sea (CAS) data.

As a result, arrivals to China in July are likely to drop from 300,100 b/d in June to about 97,100 b/d, the lowest since 95,600 b/d in December 2021, CAS data showed. It usually takes about 35 days for an ethane cargo to travel to China from the US.

The US Energy Information Administration previously projected that US ethane exports would decrease by 80,000 b/d in 2025 and by 177,000 b/d in 2026 if the licensing requirements for US exports of ethane to China remain in place.

Analysts and industrial sources said the cargoes to Chinese petchems producer Satellite Chemical are expected to contribute most of the rebound in the July loading for August delivery.CAS data showed that Jiangsu province, where Satellite Chemical is based, received an average of 203,000 b/d of US ethane over the last 12 months, July 2024 to June 2025. This accounted for more than 75% of China’s imports in the same period.Due to the US export restrictions, deliveries to the province in July were likely to slump 86% to 28,900 b/d from the average volume, according to CAS.Unlike its ethane-fed chemical peers, Satellite Chemical is wholly reliant on US ethane as feedstock.Enterprise analysts said the intense volatility in US-China ethane trade flows in recent months has led ethane-fed chemical plants to reconsider their dependency on US supplies, despite Satellite Chemical’s strong profit performance last year.Before the BIS noticed the export restriction, US ethane had been included in China’s additional tariffs list amid the trade conflict between the top economies.China’s ethane imports dropped 35% month over month to about 204,000 b/d in May due to the additional tariffs. But the volume rebounded by 47% in June following Beijing’s decision to exempt US ethane from additional tariffs in late April/early May, CAS data showed.According to Platts, China’s imported ethane-based ethylene capacity — almost all of which is independent — now stands at roughly 4 million metric tons per year.The capacity can be translated into about 248,000 b/d of imported ethane feedstocks requirement, which is spread across five plants in eastern China’s Jiangsu, Zhejiang and Shandong provinces.Unlike Satellite Chemical, the four other plants have more feedstock flexibility to process alternatives naphtha, LPG and domestically produced ethane.

data.title: Arisinfra Solutions Arm Bags Contract Worth INR1 Billion
data.description:
Integrated construction materials and services provider Arisinfra Solutions , through its subsidiary ArisUnitern RE Solutions, has secured a contract worth around 1 billion Indian rupees for a residential project commissioned by Village Wave.

The project is spread across 21.93 acres and is estimated to have a gross development value of 2.88 billion rupees, according to a Friday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

Under the pact, Arisinfra will hold the exclusive rights to supply all construction materials and provide end-to-end development management services, including project monitoring, sales, and collections for the project.

data.title: Silchester Reduces Stake in Nippon Kayaku to Below 10%
data.description:
Nippon Kayaku Co said that UK-based investment firm Silchester International Investors LLP has reduced its stake in the company, resulting in a change in major shareholder status as of July 2, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

According to a large shareholding report filed with the Kanto Local Finance Bureau, Silchester's holdings fell to 9.97% of voting rights, or 15.91 million shares, from 10.99% (17.54 million shares) previously.

The change marks Silchester's exit from the category of major shareholders holding 10% or more of voting rights.

data.title: Trinseo expands share of sustainable products in portfolio to 8%, targeting 40% by 2030
data.description:
Trinseo PLC (Wayne, Pennsylvania) expanded its sustainable product portfolio to 8.1% of its overall portfolio in 2024, up from 7.2% the previous year, as the company accelerates efforts toward its 2030 target of achieving a 40% share for its sustainable products, according to its latest annual sustainability report on July 1, as per Chemweek.

The company said in the report that it is actively developing technologies that support the transition to a circular economy, such as recycled-content plastics that leverage its dissolution and depolymerization technologies. A total of 78% of its research and development efforts were spent last year on circular economy solutions, according to the report.

Trinseo opened two chemical recycling demonstration facilities in 2024 — a polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA) depolymerization plant and an acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene (ABS) dissolution facility. These additions complement Trinseo’s existing polycarbonate (PC) dissolution capabilities, creating a comprehensive styrenics recycling platform, it said.

“With increasing global demand to establish a circular economy, our most significant strides in 2024 came from expanding our recycling technologies by advancing our PC and ABS dissolution capabilities and opening our PMMA depolymerization demonstration facility,” said Frank Bozich, president and CEO of Trinseo in the report.

Trinseo added that it also made progress in the US, having been granted a patent by the US Patent Office for its dissolution-based polymer recycling technology.

In February this year, Trinseo officially launched its dissolution-based recycled polystyrene (r-PS) in Europe for direct food contact applications.

The food-grade r-PS segment remained a niche development for the wider European r-PS market, according to market sources, pending wider recognition and adoption from brand owners on product intake. However, market sources mentioned that white, post-consumer PS waste, which could typically be yogurt pots, remained highly sought after in the market as styrenics producers’ food-grade r-PS production requires such materials as feedstocks.

Platts assessed recycled non-food-grade white r-PS at €1,280 per metric ton DDP Northwest Europe on July 1.Trinseo has been developing its recycling strength on two core elements: deploying its depolymerization and dissolution chemical recycling technologies to styrenics-focused polymers, including PS, PC, ABS and PMMA, and acquiring and securing feedstocks in the market via its recycler division, Heathland BV, which Trinseo acquired in 2022.

data.title: Rheinmetall's Latest Ammunition Order Comes From New Customer — Market Talk
data.description: Rheinmetall's latest ammunition order, valued in the hundreds of millions of euros and announced Thursday, comes from a new customer, the German arms maker clarified during a second-quarter pre-close call in the evening, Jefferies analysts Chloe Lemarie and Ben Brown say. The group had previously only said that the customer was a European member of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization. Rheinmetall expects further orders from some southeast European countries in the second half of the year, they add. Shares closed at 1,698.50 euros on Thursday. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Merdeka Copper Gold's 2H Earnings Likely to Be Significantly Stronger — Market Talk
data.description: Merdeka Copper Gold's 2H earnings are likely to be significantly stronger, thanks to concluded nickel-smelter maintenance and ramped-up new projects, Nomura's Ahmad Maghfur Usman says in a research report. The minerals producer's Pani gold project is progressing on schedule at 49% completion, with commissioning targeted for late 2025 and first production in early 2026, the analyst notes. Its Tujuh Bukit Copper prefeasibility study update, which is slated for 2H 2025, incorporates an increase in ore reserves and expanded sublevel cave throughput. However, Nomura lowers the stock's target price to IDR2,900.00 from IDR3,400.00 to reflect higher weighted average cost of capital, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 1.9% lower at IDR2,050.00. An analyst at Verdhana Sekuritas also contributed to Nomura's research report. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: German Factory Orders Fall More than Expected
data.description:
Factory orders in Germany dropped by 1.4% month-over-month in May 2025, missing market forecasts of a 0.1% decline and reversing an upwardly revised 1.6% rise in the previous month.

It was the first drop in three months, weighed heavily by a 17.7% plunge in orders for computer, electronic, and optical products following several large-scale orders in April.

Demand also weakened for electrical equipment (-6.2%) and basic metals (-5.1%).

In contrast, orders rose for fabricated metal products (18.2%) and other transport equipment (6.8%).

Capital goods orders dropped 4.1%, and intermediate goods fell 3.4%, while consumer goods rose 3.1%.

Domestic orders slumped 7.8%, offsetting a 2.9% rise in foreign demand, which was driven by a 9.0% jump in non-euro area orders, despite a 6.5% fall from the eurozone.

Excluding large-scale orders, overall demand fell 3.1%.

On a less volatile three-month basis, factory orders rose 2.1% during March–May 2025, supported in part by the 90-day trade truce.

data.title: German industrial orders fall 1.4% in May
data.description:
German industrial orders fell much more than expected in May due to a one-off effect, official data showed on Friday.

Orders declined by 1.4% on the previous month on a seasonally and calendar adjusted basis. A Reuters poll of analysts had pointed to a fall of 0.1%.

The slump was primarily due to the substantial decline of 17.7% month-on-month in orders in the computer, electronic and optical products sector, where several large-scale orders were recorded in April.

Furthermore, strong decreases were also observed in the electrical equipment and basic metals sectors.

Foreign orders rose by 2.9% on the month, with orders from the euro zone decreasing by 6.5% and orders from outside the euro area increasing by 9.0%.

Domestic orders fell by 7.8% on the month, the statistics office said.

The less volatile three-month on three-month comparison showed that new orders in the period from March to May were 2.1% higher than in the previous three months.

data.title: GMM Pfaudler Arm to Acquire Brazil's SEMCO Tecnologia; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
GMM Pfaudler , through its Brazil-based wholly owned subsidiary Pfaudler, has agreed to acquire a 100% stake in industrial machinery manufacturer SEMCO Tecnologia em Processos for $18.5 million, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The acquisition is expected to help strengthen GMM Pfaudler's mixing technologies platform and give it access to South American markets.

The transaction is expected to be completed in the second quarter of the financial year 2026.

The company's shares were up nearly 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Globaltec Formation Unit Completes Well Drilling in South Sumatra, Indonesia
data.description:
Globaltec Formation's Australian unit NuEnergy Gas has completed drilling the TE-B06001 well in its Tanjung Enim production sharing contract area in South Sumatra, Indonesia.

Gas shows at the surface confirmed coal bed methane in several seams, the company said in a Friday filing.

A progressive cavity pump has been installed to help achieve stable gas production.

Gas build-up is progressing as planned and will be sent to the processing facility once output targets are met.

data.title: JPM bullish on private markets managers with exit volumes picking up pace; upgrades CVC
data.description:
** As exit volumes the past weeks look better than feared, J.P.Morgan increases its estimates and hikes its PTs to reflect a higher amount of carried interest and investment income for EQT , CVC , and Bridgepoint

** It upgrades CVC to "overweight" from "neutral" on valuation, saying it is trading at a "material discount" to EQT and Partners

** "Overweight"-rated Bridgepoint remains JPM's darling among private market managers as it sees the most upside on the back of exit momentum and expectations of a successful fundraising of two flagships next year

** Although the broker acknowledges EQT's strong equity story after several exits announcements, it notes that it is reflected in the share price following a 42% rally

** As for Partners , JPM cuts EPS estimates by around 6% as the dollar weakens, but keeps its "overweight" rating and sees investor concerns as overblown

data.title: Turkey - Factors to watch on July 4
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira traded at 39.8400 against the U.S. dollar at 0500 GMT, compared with a close on Thursday of 39.8045.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.39% higher at 10.228.39 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Most Asian equity markets struggled on Friday, despite record highs for Wall Street overnight, as U.S. President Donald Trump's deadline for trade deals loomed next week.

ERDOGAN

President Tayyip Erdogan travels to Azerbaijan to attend the 17th Economic Cooperation Organization (ECO) Summit. He is scheduled to join the official welcome ceremony, a family photo session, the summit session, and a reception hosted by Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev.

OPPOSITION

Main opposition CHP leader Ozgur Ozel to meet with Labour Party (EMEP) leader Seyit Aslan.

TURKISH LIRA

Central Bank (TCMB) to release real effective exchange rate data for June. (1130)

INFLATION

Central Bank also to publish monthly price developments report. (1800)
